what_it_does: Defines the npm package manifest for a Vite + React + TypeScript surface application named instrument-surface, including lifecycle scripts that synchronize a gallery before dev/build and commands to run, build, and preview the app.
observations: Private ESM package at version 0.0.1 named instrument-surface
Description frames it as a paper-aesthetic instrument surface built alongside canvas/app, never on top
A sync-gallery script is invoked in both predev and prebuild hooks
dev and preview both bind to port 5174 and dev binds host 0.0.0.0
Runtime dependencies are React 18, React-DOM 18, and framer-motion ^12.4.0
Dev dependencies are Vite 6, @vitejs/plugin-react, TypeScript 5.6, and React type packages
standouts: The description explicitly says the surface is built alongside canvas/app, never on top
Gallery synchronization is wired into both predev and prebuild
Port 5174 is hardcoded for dev and preview
concerns: Version is 0.0.1 with no test, lint, or typecheck scripts
No engines or packageManager field is specified
predev/prebuild depend on a local script whose behavior is not visible here
notes: This is the manifest and build entrypoint for a React/Vite surface app that keeps gallery assets in sync before running or building.
questions: What does scripts/sync-gallery.mjs actually synchronize and from where?
What is canvas/app and how does this surface relate to it structurally?
Why is port 5174 chosen and is it coordinated with other apps?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose as a paper-aesthetic instrument surface manifest matches the file: it declares dependencies, scripts, and build tooling for that app.
intention_signals: It appears designed to establish an independent, motion-rich surface application that stays adjacent to an existing canvas/app and ensures its gallery is up to date before every dev session and production build.
novelty: The manifest frames architectural positioning in its description ('built alongside canvas/app, never on top') and bakes gallery synchronization into standard npm lifecycle hooks.
contribution: This file contributes the package identity, dependency contract, and scripted lifecycle for the surface app, including the gallery-sync pre-hooks and Vite/React/TypeScript configuration targets that a fused system would retain.
summary_for_embedding: company/surface/app/package.json is the npm manifest for instrument-surface, a private ESM Vite+React+TypeScript app that runs gallery synchronization before dev and build and serves on port 5174.
intention_for_embedding: This file exists to define the surface app's package metadata, runtime and build dependencies, and lifecycle scripts that keep a gallery synchronized while running alongside a canvas/app.
